nodes.x: I waited patiently for the Lord; and he inclined unto me, and heard my cry.
In my distress I cried unto the Lord, and he heard me.
In my distress I called upon the Lord, and cried unto my God: he heard my voice out of his temple, and my cry came before him, even into his ears.

And my soul hungered; and I kneeled down before my Maker, and I cried unto him in mighty prayer and supplication for mine own soul; and all the day long did I cry unto him; yea, and when the night came I did still raise my voice high that it reached the heavens.
I cried to thee, O Lord; and unto the Lord I made supplication.
In my distress I called upon the Lord, and cried to my God: and he did hear my voice out of his temple, and my cry did enter into his ears.
He brought me up also out of an horrible pit, out of the miry clay, and set my feet upon a rock, and established my goings.
And the spirit entered into me when he spake unto me, and set me upon my feet, that I heard him that spake unto me.
He hath inclosed my ways with hewn stone, he hath made my paths crooked.
And when he had spoken such words unto me, I set my face toward the ground, and I became dumb.
He hath also broken my teeth with gravel stones, he hath covered me with ashes.

and formed of craggy rock, while he went to reconnoitre the house, and to get something ready for me to eat. He soon returned, informing me that his brother was out and would not be in till dark, but the family wished very much that I would come in, as the children would hold their tongues, and it was thought to be perfectly safe. I declined, however, for the present, and he brought me out some…
And he hath put a new song in my mouth, even praise unto our God: many shall see it, and fear, and shall trust in the Lord.
O Lord, open thou my lips; and my mouth shall shew forth thy praise.
Praise ye the Lord. Sing unto the Lord a new song, and his praise in the congregation of saints.
O bless our God, ye people, and make the voice of his praise to be heard:
Praise ye the Lord. Blessed is the man that feareth the Lord, that delighteth greatly in his commandments.
Let every thing that hath breath praise the Lord. Praise ye the Lord.
Blessed is that man that maketh the Lord his trust, and respecteth not the proud, nor such as turn aside to lies.
Blessed is the man that trusteth in the Lord, and whose hope the Lord is.
O taste and see that the Lord is good: blessed is the man that trusteth in him.
He that trusteth in his own heart is a fool: but whoso walketh wisely, he shall be delivered.

Earning the Trust of the Lord and Your Family By Elder Richard J. Maynes Of the Seventy Men who have “integrity of heart” are men to be trusted—because trust is built on integrity. Brethren, perhaps there is no greater compliment we could receive from the Lord than to know He trusts us to be worthy priesthood holders and great husbands and fathers. One thing is for certain: earning the Lord’s…
According to the glorious gospel of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust.

Many, O Lord my God, are thy wonderful works which thou hast done, and thy thoughts which are to us-ward: they cannot be reckoned up in order unto thee: if I would declare and speak of them, they are…
O Lord, thou art my God; I will exalt thee, I will praise thy name; for thou hast done wonderful things; thy counsels of old are faithfulness and truth.
O Lord, how great are thy works! and thy thoughts are very deep.
And thy servant is in the midst of thy people which thou hast chosen, a great people, that cannot be numbered nor counted for multitude.
All thy works shall praise thee, O Lord; and thy saints shall bless thee.
I will praise thee, O Lord, with my whole heart; I will shew forth all thy marvellous works.
Sacrifice and offering thou didst not desire; mine ears hast thou opened: burnt offering and sin offering hast thou not required.
For thou desirest not sacrifice; else would I give it: thou delightest not in burnt offering.
Above when he said, Sacrifice and offering and burnt offerings and offering for sin thou wouldest not, neither hadst pleasure therein; which are offered by the law;
In burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin thou hast had no pleasure.
Thou hast not brought me the small cattle of thy burnt offerings; neither hast thou honoured me with thy sacrifices. I have not caused thee to serve with an offering, nor wearied thee with incense.
Take heed to thyself that thou offer not thy burnt offerings in every place that thou seest:
Then said I, Lo, I come: in the volume of the book it is written of me,
Then said I, Lo, I come (in the volume of the book it is written of me,) to do thy will, O God.
And behold, and lo, I come quickly to judgment, to convince all of their ungodly deeds which they have committed against me, as it is written of me in the volume of the book.
Then the word of the Lord came to me, saying,
Moreover the word of the Lord came to me, saying,
Then the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
I delight to do thy will, O my God: yea, thy law is within my heart.
I have longed for thy salvation, O Lord; and thy law is my delight.
Give me understanding, and I shall keep thy law; yea, I shall observe it with my whole heart.
Let thy tender mercies come unto me, that I may live: for thy law is my delight.
Unless thy law had been my delights, I should then have perished in mine affliction.
O how love I thy law! it is my meditation all the day.
I have preached righteousness in the great congregation: lo, I have not refrained my lips, O Lord, thou knowest.
My lips shall utter praise, when thou hast taught me thy statutes.
My tongue shall speak of thy word: for all thy commandments are righteousness.
The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable: but the mouth of the wicked speaketh frowardness.
Do ye indeed speak righteousness, O congregation? do ye judge uprightly, O ye sons of men?
I have not hid thy righteousness within my heart; I have declared thy faithfulness and thy salvation: I have not concealed thy lovingkindness and thy truth from the great congregation.
Thy word have I hid in mine heart, that I might not sin against thee.
In thee, O Lord, do I put my trust; let me never be ashamed: deliver me in thy righteousness.
Thy testimonies that thou hast commanded are righteous and very faithful.
But thou, when thou prayest, enter into thy closet, and when thou hast shut thy door, pray to thy Father who is in secret; and thy Father, who seeth in secret, shall reward thee openly.
But I have trusted in thy mercy; my heart shall rejoice in thy salvation.
Withhold not thou thy tender mercies from me, O Lord: let thy lovingkindness and thy truth continually preserve me.
Hear me, O Lord; for thy lovingkindness is good: turn unto me according to the multitude of thy tender mercies.
Have mercy upon me, O God, according to thy lovingkindness: according unto the multitude of thy tender mercies blot out my transgressions.
Let thy mercies come also unto me, O Lord, even thy salvation, according to thy word.
Great are thy tender mercies, O Lord: quicken me according to thy judgments.
For innumerable evils have compassed me about: mine iniquities have taken hold upon me, so that I am not able to look up; they are more than the hairs of mine head: therefore my heart faileth me.
Wherefore should I fear in the days of evil, when the iniquity of my heels shall compass me about?
They compassed me about; yea, they compassed me about: but in the name of the Lord I will destroy them.
For dogs have compassed me: the assembly of the wicked have inclosed me: they pierced my hands and my feet.
And it came to pass that after they had bound me insomuch that I could not move, the compass, which had been prepared of the Lord, did cease to work.
Be pleased, O Lord, to deliver me: O Lord, make haste to help me.
Make haste, O God, to deliver me; make haste to help me, O Lord.
But be not thou far from me, O Lord: O my strength, haste thee to help me.
O God, be not far from me: O my God, make haste for my help.
Make haste to help me, O Lord my salvation.
But I am poor and needy: make haste unto me, O God: thou art my help and my deliverer; O Lord, make no tarrying.
Let them be ashamed and confounded together that seek after my soul to destroy it; let them be driven backward and put to shame that wish me evil.
Let them be ashamed and confounded that seek after my soul: let them be turned backward, and put to confusion, that desire my hurt.
Let them be confounded and put to shame that seek after my soul: let them be turned back and brought to confusion that devise my hurt.
Let them be confounded and consumed that are adversaries to my soul; let them be covered with reproach and dishonour that seek my hurt.
Let them be ashamed and brought to confusion together that rejoice at mine hurt: let them be clothed with shame and dishonour that magnify themselves against me.
Let this be the reward of mine adversaries from the Lord, and of them that speak evil against my soul.
Let them be desolate for a reward of their shame that say unto me, Aha, aha.
Let them be turned back for a reward of their shame that say, Aha, aha.
Let them be confounded and troubled for ever; yea, let them be put to shame, and perish:
Give them sorrow of heart, thy curse unto them.
They shall not be ashamed in the evil time: and in the days of famine they shall be satisfied.
Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee: let such as love thy salvation say continually, The Lord be magnified.
Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee: and let such as love thy salvation say continually, Let God be magnified.
The king shall joy in thy strength, O Lord; and in thy salvation how greatly shall he rejoice!
Glory ye in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the Lord.
But let all those that put their trust in thee rejoice: let them ever shout for joy, because thou defendest them: let them also that love thy name be joyful in thee.
But I am poor and needy; yet the Lord thinketh upon me: thou art my help and my deliverer; make no tarrying, O my God.
For he shall deliver the needy when he crieth; the poor also, and him that hath no helper.
Blessed is he that considereth the poor: the Lord will deliver him in time of trouble.
Bow down thine ear, O Lord, hear me: for I am poor and needy.
All my bones shall say, Lord, who is like unto thee, which deliverest the poor from him that is too strong for him, yea, the poor and the needy from him that spoileth him?
